core_mechanism: a self-charging-energy threshold that blocks discrete, one-quantum-at-a-time transfer across a small isolated element until an external drive supplies the fixed energy cost of adding a single quantum (Coulomb-blockade-type quantized-transport gating with a sharp drive-dependent onset)
brief_reason: The abstract attributes the loss to 'Coulomb blockade and microwave-driven charge tunnelling in metallic grains,' i.e. single-charge transfer onto a small grain that is energetically forbidden until the microwave drive overcomes the grain's charging-energy threshold, which is why the loss is microwave-power-dependent.
